messages.content:
a. 3 hours
b. 4 hours
c. 1 hour
d. 2 hours
a. 3 hours

Which of the following question demonstrates application level of Bloom's taxonomy?
Select one:
a. What formula can be used to calculate the mechanical advantage of this system?
b. Describe the function of this resistor
c. Solve the resistance value in Ohm and the accuracy score in percentage of Resistor 1
d. Which chemical used in a process removes oil, grease, dirt and even paint from the iron or steel even before it gets dipped in zinc.
c. Solve the resistance value in Ohm and the accuracy score in percentage of Resistor 1

Which level of planning does lesson planning fall under?
Select one:
a. Fourth level
b. Third level
c. Second level
d. First level
c. Second level
I apologize for the confusion. Lesson planning typically falls under the first level of planning, which is operational planning.

Which of the following is not an FET study Field linked to Technology Grades 10-12?
Select one:
a. Mechanical Technology
b. Design
c. Physical Sciences
d. Life Sciences
c. Physical Sciences

Possible structures projects can be made using locally sourced resources such as:
Select one:
a. Word processing software
b. Model boats and aeroplanes
c. USB storage
d. Chip programming software
b. Model boats and airplanes

According to technology, what are resources?
Select one:
a. Things that replace teachers or undermine their integrity and dignity.
b. Learning material that replace appropriate and effective methods of learning for the sake of novelty or fashion.
c. Branded, expensive school uniform, school bags and badges.
d. Well trained, motivated teachers and school staff.
d. Well trained, motivated teachers and school staff.

What is the purpose of acoustic comfort in classroom management?
Select one:
a. To enable learners to learn and prevent lasting damage to learners' hearing.
b. To enhance teacher's communication with students.
c. To create a healthy and comfortable learning environment.
d. To increase student motivation.
c. To create a healthy and comfortable learning environment.

Which of the following is a benefit of using technology to teach technology?
Select one:
a. Students become less technologically proficient.
b. Students are exposed to outdated technology.
c. Students can build virtual models on computers.
d. Students are less likely to use technology in the future.
c. Students can build virtual models on computers.

A test-based assessment is:
Select one:
a. A type of assessment where learners assess one another in pairs or groups
b. An assessment that provides an opportunity for learners, teachers and caregivers to acknowledge a learner's progress and achievement.
c. A type of assessment where learners assess themselves against given criteria that may be reflected on as a self-checklist.
d. An assessment where the same evidence is gathered for all learners in the same way at the same time.
Question 10
d. An assessment where the same evidence is gathered for all learners in the same way at the same time.

Not yet answeredMarked out of 1Not flaggedFlag question
Question text
Which of the following best describes the difference between tools and materials and kits?
Select one:
a. Tools and materials are more expensive than kits.
b. Tools and materials are less versatile than kits.
c. Kits come with all the necessary parts, while tools and materials do not.
d. Tools and materials are used to build things, while kits are not.
c. Kits come with all the necessary parts, while tools and materials do not.

Which of the following is NOT a facility resource example
Select one:
a. Workshop
b. Lesson plan
c. Laboratory
d. Classroom
b. Lesson plan

What is the purpose of an assessments in Technology?
Select one:
a. To encourage individual work
b. To help learners to work together with their peers
c. To measure the effectiveness of ongoing teaching
d. To understand how to use the Zone of Proximal Development
c. To measure the effectiveness of ongoing teaching

Which technological skills are needed by a technology teacher?
Select one:
a. The ability to share entrepreneurial spirit with the learners.
b. The ability to manage your classroom throughout the lesson.
c. The ability to exemplify technological theory in relevant and appropriate contexts.
d. The ability to neatly write notes on the white board.
c. The ability to exemplify technological theory in relevant and appropriate contexts.

Classroom management are NOT effective when:
Select one:
a. They keep the learner quiet throughout the lesson and allow only the teacher the talk
b. They promote active learning and learner involvement
c. They emphasize learners' expectations for behaviour and learning
d. They identify important learner behaviours for success
a. They keep the learner quiet throughout the lesson and allow only the teacher to talk.

What is a MINI Practical Assessment Task?
Select one:
a. A task which makes up the main formal assessment of a learner's skills and knowledge application during each term.
b. A task that learners do and submit at the end of the year as the portfolio of evidence.
c. It is a class activity that learners write after a lesson
d. Activities used to teach and then practice specific skills in preparation for a more advanced task sometimes also called resource tasks.
d. Activities used to teach and then practice specific skills in preparation for a more advanced task sometimes also called resource tasks.

Question 16
Not yet answeredMarked out of 1Not flaggedFlag question
Question text
One of the statements is untrue about healthy and active learning when using computers.
Select one:
a. Positioning light of visual display unit screens to reduce interference from reflected light.
b. Restricting usage and taking regular breaks.
c. Ensuring correct positioning of computers.
d. Using any kind of a seat when using a computer.
d. Using any kind of a seat when using a computer.

Which statement is untrue about an inclusive approach to learners' needs for ideal classroom planning
Select one:
a. The teacher must foster a positive and respectful classroom culture
b. The teacher must get to know their students
c. The teacher must Offer one mode of instruction
d. The teacher must involve learners by recognizing the unique needs and strengths of each learner
c. The teacher must offer one mode of instruction.

As Technology teachers, how can you gather information where measurement is concerned?
Select one:
a. Bringing learning materials and equipment in class
b. Using the Zone of Proximal Development
c. Using Bloom's taxonomy
d. Observation
d. Observation

According to the Department of Education, which one is the specific aims of technology?
Select one:
a. For learners to be prepared to answer exam and tests questions.
b. To help learners understand calculations.
c. For the development and application of specific design skills to solve problems.
d. To improve the standards of living.
c. For the development and application of specific design skills to solve problems.

What is the purpose of atmosphere and thermal comfort in classroom management?
Select one:
a. To create a quiet and sterile learning environment that minimizes distractions.
b. To create a positive and supportive learning environment that promotes students' physical and emotional well-being, and helps them learn effectively.
c. To create a competitive learning environment that motivates students to outperform each other.
d. To create a challenging learning environment that pushes students to their limits.
b. To create a positive and supportive learning environment that promotes students' physical and emotional well-being, and helps them learn effectively.

At the stage of lesson conclusion, what is one of the things that a technology teacher does?
Select one:
a. Follows on the additional concepts and content that was introduced, internalized and interpreted.
b. Prepares to take the lesson further by explaining the concepts.
c. Introduce new concepts and go deeper into content.
d. Ask baseline questions to check how much learners know about the subject.
a. Follows on the additional concepts and content that was introduced, internalized and interpreted.

Which of the following is NOT promoted in groupwork?
Select one:
a. Co-operative learning
b. Collaborative learning
c. Teamwork
d. Autonomy
d. Autonomy

Which level of planning does learning programme fall under?
Select one:
a. Fourth level
b. Second level
c. First level
d. Third level
d. Third level
